codeReview.summary: The geocoder expansion materially improves coverage and the new tests exercise the expected regions and aliases. The implementation is broadly sound, though one alias-resolution gap remains in the exact country-to-ISO lookup path used by the source catalog.

issues.description: countryToIso() still performs a direct COUNTRY_TO_ISO lookup and never consults COUNTRY_ALIASES, so alternate country strings such as "Congo-Brazzaville" are still unresolved in callers like server/sourceCatalog.js:81. In the final catalog, the feed src/services/rssService.js:177 (aa-cg) ends up with a null isoA2 even though COUNTRY_ALIASES already maps "congo brazzaville" to the canonical "Congo" entry.
issues.suggestedFix: Normalize countryToIso() through the alias map (or require catalog entries with alias country names to supply explicit isoA2 values) so source-catalog normalization benefits from the alias expansion work.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Source-catalog normalization depends on exact countryToIso() matches; alias-like country strings in feed metadata silently produce null isoA2 values unless the entry uses a canonical geocoder country name or explicit isoA2.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: server/sourceCatalog.js:81 uses countryToIso(feed.country) directly, and the final catalog still leaves aa-cg unresolved because the feed country string is "Congo-Brazzaville".

summary: Pass with a non-blocking alias-normalization gap. The geocoder expansion works overall, but exact country-to-ISO resolution still misses some alias strings.
